bio.name: Dinuja Thishean
bio.title: Cyber Security Undergraduate | SLIIT
bio.description: I am a Cyber Security undergraduate focused on secure software, vulnerability analysis, and practical web security. I build real-world projects and continuously strengthen both defensive and development skills.

about.headline: Building secure, reliable, and user-focused digital solutions.
about.summary: I combine cyber security fundamentals with hands-on web development to create projects that are both functional and resilient. My interests include secure coding, vulnerability assessment, and continuous learning through practical implementation.
highlights: Focused on cyber security and secure application development
Hands-on project experience with Python, Flask, Java, and web technologies
Strong collaboration and communication through academic and committee work

education.institution: Sri Lanka Institute of Information Technology

extracurricular.intro: National-level karate competitor with podium finishes from district and Western Province championships through the Sri Lanka National Championship—and leadership as school karate team captain.

experiences.title: SLIIT XStream 4.0
bullets: Volunteered on the finance track for SLIIT XStream 4.0—an IEEE-driven signature event—helping secure sponsorships, follow up with partners, and keep budgets transparent while the main programme showcased innovation on stage.
Worked alongside committee leads to align sponsor deliverables with event timelines so funding, perks, and printed collateral arrived before doors opened.

experiences.context: Sri Dharmaloka Central College · Commerce Society
experiences.title: Commerce Day
experiences.role: Commerce Society member · Media & English announcer
bullets: Served as an active Commerce Society member for Commerce Day—conceptualising, filming, and editing short-form videos that promoted the build-up and captured the atmosphere across our channels.
Stepped on stage as the English announcer—scripting transitions, welcoming guests, and keeping the programme flowing from opening remarks to the closing thank-you.

projects.title: SOC Dashboard — Security Operations Center
projects.description: A full-stack SOC-style dashboard that simulates how analysts monitor alerts, triage incidents, review suspicious IPs, and read trends from live-style metrics and charts—built to mirror real security operations workflows.
projects.role: Full-stack developer · Cyber security & web application focus
outcomes: Delivers an end-to-end analyst-style workflow from alert noise to incident context—not just static pages
Surfaces severity, brute-force patterns, and IP context in a layout recruiters and hiring managers recognize from real SOCs
Demonstrates comfort with modern front-end dashboards plus API and data-layer design for security use cases

modalDetails.problem: Security operations teams need a single place to see what is noisy versus what is urgent—alerts, user activity, suspicious endpoints, and trends—without drowning in raw logs.
modalDetails.whatItDoes: This project is a Security Operations Center–inspired dashboard: it organizes alerts by severity, tracks incidents through a practical workflow, highlights suspicious IP behaviour, surfaces failed-login and brute-force style patterns, and visualizes attack trends and severity mix with charts—similar to what you would explain in an internship or junior SOC interview.
keyFeatures: Security alerts with severity levels and quick triage cues
Incident management flow from open to contained (workflow-oriented UI)
Suspicious IP tracking and context for review
Failed login / brute-force style detection narrative tied to activity feeds
Live-style activity feed plus KPI-style metric cards
Cyber security themed UI tuned for dark environments and long sessions
Charts for attack trends and severity distribution (Recharts or equivalent)
modalDetails.techSummary: Front end: React with Vite and Tailwind CSS for a fast, component-driven UI. Back end: Node.js and Express with Prisma for structured data access. Database: SQLite or PostgreSQL depending on deployment. Visualization: Recharts (or a similar chart library) for trend and severity views.
modalDetails.impact: It shows you can translate SOC concepts into software: monitoring, triage, and communication—not only coursework, but something you can walk through with recruiters or technical interviewers in cyber security and IT security roles.

projects.title: Portfolio Website
projects.description: A recruiter-ready static portfolio with a polished dark/light UI, JSON-driven content, project storytelling, and smooth navigation—built to load fast and stay easy to update.
projects.role: Owner & developer · HTML, CSS, JavaScript, static hosting
outcomes: Shipped a single-page experience with structured sections for skills, education, projects, and contact
Shipped theme persistence and responsive layout patterns that work from mobile to desktop
Deployed as a static site so updates are simple: edit data, push, and the site reflects the change

modalDetails.problem: Recruiters skim quickly—you need a clear story, strong projects, and contact paths without a heavy framework or slow load times.
modalDetails.whatItDoes: This site is a static portfolio: it presents your profile, skills, certifications, experience, and projects in one scrollable flow. Content is driven from JSON so you can update copy and links without rebuilding a whole app, and the UI supports light and dark themes for long reading sessions.
keyFeatures: Single-page layout with anchor navigation and scroll-friendly sections
JSON-driven project and profile content for quick edits
Project filters, screenshot galleries in a modal, and CV download support
Dark/light theme with persisted preference
Responsive layout tuned for phones, tablets, and desktops
Deployed as static files for speed and straightforward hosting
modalDetails.techSummary: Plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript—no build step required for visitors. Content loads from data.json; styling uses CSS variables for theming. Hosted on Vercel (or similar) as a static deployment.
modalDetails.impact: It is the storefront for your work: internships and hiring managers see your projects, stack, and contact details in one place—professional, fast, and easy to keep current.

projects.title: Automated Vulnerability Scanner
projects.description: A Flask-powered security tool that automates common web vulnerability checks and presents results in a way that supports triage—built to practice offensive-style testing workflows responsibly.
outcomes: Reduced repetitive manual checks by encoding repeatable tests in a small web workflow
Improved scan readability so findings are easier to prioritize and discuss
Captured practical security workflows with screenshots for portfolio and review

modalDetails.problem: Manual probing is slow and inconsistent when you are learning web security—you need a structured way to run baseline checks and compare results over time.
modalDetails.whatItDoes: This project wraps common vulnerability testing ideas in a Flask application: you trigger scans through a web-style workflow and review output that is organized for triage. It is a learning and demonstration tool for responsible security practice—not a replacement for enterprise scanning.
keyFeatures: Automated test flows for common web weakness patterns
Web-oriented interface for running scans and reading output
Output formatted to support quick review and follow-up
Screenshot-friendly views for documentation and portfolio use
modalDetails.techSummary: Python with Flask for routing and server-side logic, plus HTML-oriented reporting surfaces. The focus is on clarity of workflow and results rather than a large distributed architecture.
modalDetails.impact: Shows you can connect security concepts to working code: how tests are triggered, how results are presented, and how a reviewer would actually use the tool—valuable signal for security-minded internships and junior roles.

projects.title: Hotel Reservation System
projects.description: A full-stack academic hotel booking platform with Spring Boot services, REST-style APIs, and MySQL persistence—focused on clean reservation flows and maintainable Java structure.
projects.role: Full-stack developer · academic team / individual project
outcomes: Implemented reservation and room management flows across UI and service layers
Practiced layered Spring Boot design with practical CRUD operations
Strengthened database modeling and API thinking for real-world booking scenarios

modalDetails.problem: Booking systems need consistent rules: room availability, guest data, and updates must stay in sync across the UI and database—academic projects often oversimplify that story.
modalDetails.whatItDoes: This project models a hotel reservation domain with Spring Boot on the server side and MySQL for persistence. The focus is on structured REST interactions, sensible layering, and CRUD paths that mirror how a small property might manage rooms and reservations.
keyFeatures: Reservation and room management workflows
REST API surface for core operations
MySQL-backed persistence and practical schema thinking
Java/Spring Boot structure suitable for extension and testing
modalDetails.techSummary: Java with Spring Boot for application services, REST endpoints for client interaction, and MySQL as the relational store. The stack reflects common enterprise-style web service patterns.
modalDetails.impact: Demonstrates backend fundamentals that transfer beyond hospitality: transactional thinking, API design, and data modeling—useful when interviewers ask for examples beyond front-end work.
